title: Mega Venusaur Battle Guide
summary: A bulky [Grass]/[Poison] special [tank] that turns its worst weaknesses into resistances through [thick-fat] and grinds opponents down with [recover]y loops.
strengths: 123/120 mixed bulk [absorb]s both sides of the board
weaknesses: 122 SpA lacks finishing power against bulky teams
The pre-Mega turn can still eat an unexpected weakness hit

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [Grass] STAB that converts damage into healing
Secondary STAB that chips Fairies and mirror [Grass]
Status card that punishes defensive pivots
Closes the [recover]y loop and forces long-game decisions

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone — there is no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Venusaur is the defensive opposite of its [chlorophyll] base form. [thick-fat] takes its worst offensive weaknesses — [Fire] and [Ice] — and halves them, while 123/120 mixed bulk turns it into a wall that can hold both sides of the field. Speed 80 is still slow, so the plan is never trading blows — it is outlasting opponents through recovery and status.

## Strengths
[thick-fat] is the whole identity: it effectively erases [Ice] and [Fire] coverage from the metagame's top special attackers, letting Mega Venusaur switch into moves that would obliterate almost any other [Grass] type. 122 SpA keeps it dangerous enough that defensive switch-ins cannot ignore it, and the [leech-seed] + [giga-drain] + leftovers loop regenerates absurd amounts of HP across a match. Status immunity via good special bulk also makes it resistant to [poison] and burn grinders.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[psychic], [Flying], and [Ground] all still come through for 2x, and fast [psychic] sweepers or [earthquake] spammers can force it out with a single hit. Its low offense means setup sweepers behind it win the long game, so [sleep-powder] and [leech-seed] must be used proactively to steal turns. The pre-Mega entry turn lacks [thick-fat], which means a telegraphed [fire-blast] switch-in can still cost the match — time the Mega Evolution around predictable pivots.

## Recommended Moveset
[giga-drain] is locked in as the primary STAB because its healing is the backbone of the loop. [sludge-bomb] is the second STAB that chips Fairies and mirror [Grass], and the [poison] proc adds to the long-game plan. The third slot is [sleep-powder], which forces defensive opponents to switch blind and converts passive turns into free pressure. The last slot is [leech-seed], which completes the recovery loop — this four-move set wins most [tank]-versus-[tank] matchups by attrition.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[venusaurite] is the only item — the Mega Stone is mandatory. [thick-fat] is locked automatically upon Mega Evolution, so the ability slot is a non-choice. For Tera, [Water] is the cleanest option because it patches [Grass], [Ice], [Fire], and [Ground] all at once, while [Steel] flips [Poison] and [psychic] into resist/immune range. Offensive Tera [Grass] is a poor fit — this build never wants to trade HP for damage.

## Teammates & Synergy
Mega Venusaur is the special pivot of the team, so pair it with a physical wall ([Steel] or [Fairy]) to split defensive duties. Always carry a dedicated [Dark]-type switch-in to handle the [psychic] weakness, and run a fast cleaner behind it to convert stall turns into kills. A [taunt] user is also valuable insurance against opposing setup sweepers who would otherwise freely boost into it.

## Play Patterns
Switch in on turn two, lead with [leech-seed], follow with [sleep-powder], and save Mega Evolution for a predicted switch. Only use [giga-drain] below 60% HP to avoid wasted healing overflow.
